steps.title: Compute the discriminant
steps.content: For a quadratic equation, the discriminant determines how many real solutions there are. Here $a=1$, $b=-14$, and $c=40$, so compute $$(-14)^2-4(1)(40)=196-160=36$$

steps.title: Interpret the sign
steps.content: A positive discriminant means the equation has two distinct real solutions. Since $36>0$, the given quadratic has exactly two distinct real solutions. The answer is A.
